title: Jacobaea arenaria
intro: Jacobaea arenaria, also known as Corn Marigold or False Corn Marigold, is a flowering plant species within the daisy family (Asteraceae). This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its health benefits, which include treating wounds, reducing inflammation, and possessing antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Jacobaea arenaria include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Jacobaea arenaria include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to support skin health and immune function.
